seoTitle: How to Connect Jira With Power BI for Agile Reporting
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Jira with Power BI to build agile reporting dashboards that track team performance, sprint progress, and project bottlenecks.
intro: Jira tracks the day-to-day of agile teams well, but turning that data into shareable insight often means stepping outside Jira itself. This guide walks through connecting Jira with Power BI to build dashboards suited for agile reporting. It's designed for project managers and analysts who want clearer visibility into sprint progress and team performance.

steps.heading: Configure Jira for the connection
steps.body: Make sure you have the necessary API access or credentials in Jira, since Power BI will need permission to read issue and project data.

steps.heading: Connect Power BI to Jira
steps.body: In Power BI Desktop, set up the connection using a suitable connector or the Jira REST API, entering your site details and authenticating.

steps.heading: Generate reports from Jira data
steps.body: Build visuals around fields like issue status, sprint dates, and assignees to track project progress and identify where work is getting stuck.

steps.heading: Apply use cases for agile reporting
steps.body: Use the dashboard for scenarios like tracking sprint burndown, comparing team velocity over time, or spotting recurring bottlenecks in the workflow.

steps.heading: Plan your next steps
steps.body: Once the basics are working, consider adding automated refreshes so your agile dashboards reflect the latest sprint activity without manual updates.

faq.question: Do I need Jira admin access to connect it to Power BI?
faq.answer: You generally need at least API access with permission to read the relevant projects, though full admin rights aren't always required depending on your Jira setup.

faq.question: Can Power BI show sprint burndown charts using Jira data?
faq.answer: Yes, with the right fields imported, you can recreate burndown-style visuals in Power BI, giving more customization than Jira's native charts.

faq.question: How current is the Jira data shown in Power BI?
faq.answer: It reflects whatever was pulled at the last refresh, so scheduling regular refreshes keeps the dashboard aligned with ongoing sprint activity.

faq.question: Can I combine data from multiple Jira projects in one dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you have access, you can import and combine issue data from several projects into a single Power BI report for portfolio-level views.
